Home Projects Publications Blog About

About Me

Hi, I'm Wenjin Tao - an engineer, researcher, and product leader focused on intelligent manufacturing. My work lives where robotics, AI, and factory operations meet, and I am most interested in building systems that are technically sound, operationally useful, and realistic to deploy.

What I’m Working On

Currently, I am:

  • Leading product work focused on manufacturing automation and intelligent systems
  • Exploring agentic AI, digital twins, and workflow tools for industrial settings
  • Looking for better ways to connect advanced technology with actual operator and factory needs

Past Experience

In 2012, I co-founded MeoryTech, where we designed and built desktop 3D printers and supporting software. We worked with research institutes and consulting firms, which gave me an early education in product design, execution, and the difference between a clever prototype and a system people will pay to use.

That experience shaped how I think about scope, adoption, and cross-functional work to this day.

Research Background

I earned a PhD in Mechanical Engineering from Missouri University of Science and Technology. My research centered on worker-centered intelligent manufacturing, including multimodal sensing, human activity recognition, and intelligent systems that support people on the factory floor.

Outside of Work

Outside of work, I like exploring ideas that cross disciplines. Lately that has included music production, writing, and following the odd connections between engineering, culture, and everyday life.

Building product strategy and factory-grade AI at the intersection of robotics, computer vision, and manufacturing.

© 2026 Wenjin Tao
